text: Lesson 43
zh: 第43课

text: Fully insured
zh: 全额保险

text: Who owned the pie dish and why?
zh: 馅饼盘是谁的？为什么？

text: Insurance companies are normally willing to insure anything.
zh: 保险公司通常愿意为任何东西投保。

text: Insuring public or private property is a standard practice in most countries in the world.
zh: 为公共或私人财产投保是世界上大多数国家的常见做法。

text: If, however, you were holding an open air garden party or a fete it would be equally possible to insure yourself in the event of bad weather.
zh: 然而，如果你举办一场露天花园派对或游园会，同样也可以为恶劣天气投保。

text: Needless to say, the bigger the risk an insurance company takes, the higher the premium you will have to pay.
zh: 不用说，保险公司承担的风险越大，你需要支付的保费就越高。

text: It is not uncommon to hear that a shipping company has made a claim for the cost of salvaging a sunken ship.
zh: 听说一家航运公司为打捞沉船的费用提出索赔，这并不罕见。

text: But the claim made by a local authority to recover the cost of salvaging a sunken pie dish must surely be unique.
zh: 但地方政府为打捞一个沉没的馅饼盘而提出索赔，这一定是绝无仅有的。

text: Admittedly it was an unusual pie dish, for it was eighteen feet long and six feet wide.
zh: 不可否认，这是一个不寻常的馅饼盘，因为它长十八英尺，宽六英尺。

text: It had been purchased by a local authority so that an enormous pie could be baked for an annual fair.
zh: 这是当地政府购买的，以便为一年一度的集市烤制一个巨大的馅饼。

text: The pie committee decided that the best way to transport the dish would be by canal so they insured it for the trip.
zh: 馅饼委员会决定，运输这个盘子最好的方式是通过运河，因此他们为这次运输投了保。

text: Shortly after it was launched, the pie committee went to a local inn to celebrate.
zh: 盘子下水后不久，馅饼委员会就去当地一家小酒馆庆祝了。

text: At the same time, a number of teenagers climbed on to the dish and held a little party of their own.
zh: 与此同时，几个青少年爬上了盘子，自己开起了小派对。

text: Dancing proved to be more than the dish could bear,
zh: 跳舞可不是这个盘子能承受得住的，

text: for during the party it capsized and sank in seven feet of water.
zh: 因为在派对进行中，它翻了个身，沉入了七英尺深的水中。

text: The pie committee telephoned a local garage owner who arrived in a recovery truck to salvage the pie dish.
zh: 馅饼委员会打电话给当地一家车行的老板，他开着一辆救援卡车赶来打捞馅饼盘。

text: Shivering in their wet clothes, the teenagers looked on while three men dived repeatedly into the water to locate the dish.
zh: 浑身湿透、瑟瑟发抖的青少年们站在一旁，看着三名男子一次次潜入水中寻找盘子。

text: They had little difficulty in finding it, but hauling it out of the water proved to be a serious problem.
zh: 他们没费多大力气就找到了盘子，但要把它拖出水面却成了一个大难题。

text: The sides of the dish were so smooth that it was almost impossible to attach hawsers and chains to the rim without damaging it.
zh: 盘子的边缘非常光滑，几乎不可能在不损坏边缘的情况下系上缆绳和链条。

text: Eventually chains were fixed to one end of the dish and a powerful winch was put into operation.
zh: 最后，他们把链条固定在盘子的一端，启动了一台强力绞盘。

text: The dish rose to the surface and was gently drawn towards the canal bank.
zh: 盘子浮出了水面，被缓缓拖向运河岸边。

text: For one agonizing moment, the dish was perched precariously on the bank of the canal,
zh: 就在那令人揪心的一刻，盘子摇摇欲坠地停在运河岸边，

text: but it suddenly overbalanced and slid back into the water.
zh: 但它突然失去平衡，滑回了水中。

text: The men were now obliged to try once more.
zh: 这几个人现在不得不再试一次。

text: This time they fixed heavy metal clamps to both sides of the dish so that they could fasten the chains.
zh: 这一次，他们在盘子两侧都装上了沉重的金属夹钳，以便固定链条。

text: The dish now had to be lifted vertically because one edge was resting against the side of the canal.
zh: 由于盘子的一边靠在运河堤岸上，现在必须垂直吊起。

text: The winch was again put into operation and one of the men started up the truck.
zh: 绞盘再次启动，其中一人发动了卡车。

text: Several minutes later, the dish was successfully hauled above the surface of the water.
zh: 几分钟后，盘子被成功地拖出了水面。

text: streamed in torrents over its sides with such force that it set up a huge wave in the canal.
zh: 水从盘子两侧奔涌而下，力道之大，在运河里掀起了一个大浪。

text: There was a danger that the wave would rebound off the other side of the bank and send the dish plunging into the water again.
zh: 人们担心这个浪会从对岸反弹回来，把盘子再次冲入水中。

text: By working at tremendous speed, the men managed to get the dish on to dry land before the wave returned.
zh: 几个人以惊人的速度行动，赶在浪头返回之前，把盘子弄上了岸。
